mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals household consumption momentum, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample instances, or walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that triggers recession expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The trigger fires but positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances, or the signal Sharpe ratio is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below one percent combined with consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) annualized above two percent creates a deeply negative real interest rate environment that forces capital reallocation into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18 and CPIAUCSL at 220.252 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Joint signal instances fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (p > 0.1) in a bivariate linear model.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns when the unemployment rate is below six percent because supply expansions matter more when labor markets are tight; when unemployment is high, the signal weakens or reverses.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.8756 and UNRATE at 9.4 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when UNRATE is below 6 percent (Rank IC < 0.03), or the signal strength is statistically indistinguishable from its strength when UNRATE exceeds 6 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal residential investment momentum that propagates through construction and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of instances when HOUST rises for three consecutive months, or the signal underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A one-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 45 percent of instances when the trigger fires, or the conditional mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals a profit-margin squeeze as production costs rise relative to output prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change < 0% and RSAFS monthly change > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.8756 and RSAFS at 380869.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: The joint divergence signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction underperforms the simple INDPRO decline signal alone by more than 2 percentage points in hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 150 thousand persons predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days only when the unemployment rate exceeds seven percent, as job gains carry stronger marginal sentiment impact during high-unemployment regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130712.0 and UNRATE at 9.4 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above the threshold fails to produce positive 5-day returns when UNRATE exceeds 7 percent (hit rate < 55 percent), or the signal is statistically stronger when UNRATE is below 7 percent.
